message: recent reply claims work is done/fixed/passing without a verification tool call in the last 5 tool uses — run a fresh check (test, build, lint, curl, etc.) and quote its output

pattern: File has not been read yet
message: 2+ Edit/Write attempts failed with 'File has not been read yet' — Read the target file with the Read tool before editing; bash cat/sed output does not register as a read

claimed a VISUAL / rendered output (chart, diagram, UI, PDF, image) looks correct without LOOKING at it — text-only checks miss occlusion, flattening, overlap, and scale. Open or screenshot the rendered artifact with a vision-capable tool (preview / screenshot, or Read the saved image) and verify it visually, or say it is unverified.

your last turn ended on a PLAN or a PROMISE ('I'll…/let me…/next I'll…') with no completion and no tool call — anti-early-stop: if your final paragraph is a plan or a promise, do that work NOW instead of yielding. Pause only for a genuine blocker — a destructive/irreversible action, a real scope change, or input only the user can give.

you claimed the task is finished but did not ASK the user to confirm closure — do not self-close. Enumerate every distinct thing the user requested — INCLUDING every QUESTION they asked (state the answer, or why it is unanswered — not just adjacent work you did) — map each item to what you did (with verification evidence) or note why it is deferred, then explicitly ask the user whether the task can be closed.
